name: Thieves' Guild Intrigue
author: Claude
description: Urban rogues, poisoned blades, and underworld contacts for city-based campaigns.

monsters.name: Guild Enforcer
monsters.description: A scarred veteran of the underground, sent to collect debts or silence problems. Fights dirty and without mercy.

attacks.name: Hand Crossbow
attacks.specialEffect: First shot is poisoned: DC 12 CON or 1d6 extra poison damage

abilities.name: Dirty Fighter
abilities.description: Once per encounter, can throw sand/dirt: DC 12 DEX or blinded for 1 round.

abilities.name: Underworld Connections
abilities.description: If allowed to flee, will return with 1d4 reinforcements within 1 hour.

monsters.name: Shadow Dancer
monsters.description: An elite assassin trained in an ancient art that lets them step between shadows. They strike without warning and vanish without trace.

abilities.name: Shadow Step
abilities.description: Can teleport between any two areas of dim light or darkness within near range as a free action.

abilities.name: Assassinate
abilities.description: Deals an extra 2d6 damage against surprised targets or targets that haven't acted yet.

abilities.name: Evasion
abilities.description: Takes no damage on successful DEX saves, half on failure.

monsters.name: Rat King
monsters.description: A massive, intelligent rat the size of a dog, ruling a swarm of lesser rats in the sewers. Covered in matted fur and old scars.

attacks.name: Diseased Bite
attacks.specialEffect: DC 11 CON or diseased: disadvantage on CON checks for 24 hours

abilities.name: Rat Swarm
abilities.description: Surrounded by a swarm of rats. Melee attackers take 1d4 damage from biting rats.

abilities.name: Flee Through Cracks
abilities.description: Can squeeze through any gap a normal rat could fit through.

monsters.name: Corrupt City Guard
monsters.description: A guard on the guild's payroll. Will look the other way for coin, or frame adventurers if paid enough.

abilities.name: Call for Backup
abilities.description: Can blow a whistle to summon 1d4 additional guards in 1d4 rounds.

abilities.name: Bribable
abilities.description: Will accept 10+ gold to ignore crimes or share information.

spells.name: Shadow Veil
spells.description: You blend into shadows, becoming nearly invisible in dim light. Advantage on stealth checks. Attacking or casting another spell ends the effect.

effects.description: Advantage on stealth in dim light

spells.name: Whisper Wind
spells.description: Send a whispered message of up to 25 words to a creature you can see within far range. Only the target can hear it. They can whisper a reply.

effects.description: Send and receive a whispered message

weapons.name: Garrote Wire
weapons.description: Thin wire with wooden handles. Can only be used on surprised or grappled targets. Silences the target while applied.

weapons.name: Cane Sword
weapons.description: A slender blade concealed inside a gentleman's walking cane. DC 15 WIS to notice it's a weapon before it's drawn.

gear.name: Master Lockpick Set
gear.description: A fine set of picks, rakes, and tension wrenches. +1 bonus to lockpicking attempts. Breaks on a natural 1.

gear.name: Smoke Bomb
gear.description: Fills close range with thick smoke for 3 rounds. All creatures in the smoke are effectively blinded. 3 per slot.

gear.name: Serpent Venom Vial
gear.description: Apply to a weapon as an action. Next hit deals an extra 2d4 poison damage. DC 13 CON or poisoned for 1 hour.

gear.name: Disguise Kit
gear.description: Wigs, makeup, and prosthetics. Spend 10 minutes to create a disguise. Observers must pass DC 14 WIS to see through it.

gear.name: Silk Rope & Hook
gear.description: 30 feet of lightweight silk rope with a folding grappling hook. Half the weight of normal rope. Nearly silent when thrown.

backgrounds.name: Street Pickpocket
backgrounds.description: You grew up lifting purses in crowded markets. Your fingers are quick and your instincts for trouble are sharper.

backgrounds.name: Black Market Fence
backgrounds.description: You dealt in stolen goods, knowing the value of everything and the provenance of nothing. You have contacts in every market district.

backgrounds.name: Guild Informant
backgrounds.description: You sold secrets to whoever paid most. Everyone trusts you just enough to talk, and no one trusts you enough to turn their back.
